program.name: YouthWorks
program.description:
YouthWorks connects young people in Massachusetts with paid jobs. Participants work across many fields, including healthcare, education, technology, and the arts.

Jobs run in summer and during the school year, depending on the region. Participants earn an hourly wage. They also receive job readiness training and mentorship.

YouthWorks serves youth from underserved communities and those facing employment barriers. Common barriers include housing instability, limited English, disability, and justice involvement. No prior work experience needed.

Local providers run YouthWorks through MassHire workforce regions across Massachusetts. Availability and application steps vary by region — use the link above to find your local provider and confirm current openings.
program.description_short: Paid jobs and training for Massachusetts youth
program.website_description: Paid jobs and training for Massachusetts youth

documents.text: Proof of home address (ex: lease, utility bill, or school enrollment record)
Proof of identity and age (ex: school ID, state ID, passport, or birth certificate)
